questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $A=\left|\begin{array}{lll}a & b & c \\ b & c & a \\ c & a & b\end{array}\right|$ such that $|A|=0$. If $a, b, c$ are distinct, then the sum of the coordinates of the fixed point through which the line $a x+b y+c=0$ passes is
questions.solution:
A upon simplification $(a+b+c)(a-b)(b-c)(c-a)$
The only way this can be 0 is $(a+b+c)=0$
$\Rightarrow$ fixed point is $(1,1)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

If $\Delta(x)=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}e^x & \sin 2 x & \tan x^2 \\ \ln (1+x) & \cos x & \sin x \\ \cos x^2 & e^x-1 & \sin x^2\end{array}\right|=A+B x+C x^2+\ldots$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \Delta^{\prime}(x)=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}e^x & 2 \cos 2 x & 2 x \sec ^2 x^2 \\ \ln (1+x) & \cos x & \sin x \\ \cos x^2 & e^x-1 & \sin x^2\end{array}\right|+\left|\begin{array}{ccc}e^x & \sin 2 x & \tan x^2 \\ \frac{1}{(1+x)} & -\sin x & \cos x \\ \cos x^2 & e^x-1 & \sin x^2\end{array}\right|+\left|\begin{array}{ccc}e^x & \sin 2 x & \tan x^2 \\ \ln (1+x) & \cos x & \sin x \\ -2 x \sin x^2 & e^x & 2 x \cos x^2\end{array}\right| \\ & =B+2 C x+\ldots . \\ & \text { Put } x=0, B=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 2 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & 0\end{array}\right|+\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 0 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & -1 \\ 1 & 0 & 0\end{array}\right|+\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0\end{array}\right|=0\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
If three equations are consistent
$(a+1)^3 x+(a+2)^3 y=(a+3)^3$
$(a+1) x+(a+2) y=a+3$
$x+y=1$, then $a$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Since the equations are consistent
$\therefore \mathrm{D}=0$

$\left|\begin{array}{ccc}(a+1)^3 & (a+2)^3 & -(a+3)^3 \\ (a+1) & (a+2) & -(a+3) \\ 1 & 1 & -1\end{array}\right|=0$

$\begin{aligned} & \text { Put } u=(a+1), v=a+2, w=a+3 \\ & u-v=-1, v-w z-1, w-u=2 \\ & u+v+w=3 a+6\end{aligned}$

$\therefore\left|\begin{array}{ccc}u^3 & v^3 & w^3 \\ u & v & w \\ 1 & 1 & 1\end{array}\right|=0$

$\begin{aligned} & (u-v)(v-w)(w-u)(u+v+w)=0 \\ & (-1)(-1)(2)(3 a+6)=0 \\ & \text { i.e. } a=-2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
